ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Continued To Aug-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges.

Mr. Siracusa stated that they need the other Parent or Guardian to open an account, that his Client will then deposit the funds, that the Mother is in Texas and that she is difficult to get a hold of. Mr. Siracusa also stated that the Mother went to the Bank to open the account, but there was a problem and requested a 60-day continuance. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is continued to August 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges

Ms. Alavi requested a 60 day continuance, stating that Mr. Hodges is working with Jacoby's (minor) mother to open a bank account at Wells Fargo Bank in rural Texas, she was unable to open it because the bank is requiring a birth certificate, the plan is to direct the Wells Fargo in Texas to open a blocked account, and Mr. Hodges will deposit the funds in San Francisco. The status hearing on Filing of Receipt for Blocked Account is continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To May-09-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for petitioner Dwight Benton; Elizabeth Hanowsky for petitioner Thomas Lucas.

The Court inquired about the readiness of the matter for trial. Ms. Ebey replied that the parties had just exchanged information within the past week. Ms. Hanowsky stated that she had just received documents from Ms. Ebey and that she needed some time to review them; she requested a two-week continuance of the status hearing. The Court stated that he was willing to give a brief continuance of the status hearing, but that he wanted to set a trial date at today's hearing. After a brief discussion regarding proposed trial dates, the Court set the matter for trial on May 9, 2016, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 206. In response to the Court's inquiry regarding mediation, Ms. Ebey requested a shorter timeline, and the Court set February 22, 2016 as the deadline for completion of mediation. The status hearing was continued to February 29, 2016, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported).

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Feb-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-23-2015 Continued To Dec-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Dwight Benton. Elisabeth Hanowsky for Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked for a status of discovery and settlement. Ms. Ebey stated that she just received documents from the heir company, that Mr. Benton knew his uncle well and went through all of his papers and that they need a continuance to share what they have found with counsel. The two petitions for letters of administration were continued to December 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-23-2015 Continued To Dec-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Continued To Nov-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Dwight Benton who is present in court. Elisabeth Hanowsky for (petitioner) Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked if Mr. Benton contests the allegations that his uncle is survived by sisters. Ms. Ebey stated that he does, that they have not received any documents and that the petitioner was close to his uncle. The Court asked if the parties were ready for an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Hanowsky reported that they are working with the heir finding firm Brandenberg & Davis, that they believe there are sisters living in rural Texas and that they are still trying to obtain documents. The Court asked if they would like to set an evidentiary hearing or continue the matter for status on discovery. The parties agreed to continue the matter for 60 days to exchange documents and work towards a resolution. The Court stated that the parties need to file supplements per the request of the probate examiner. Ms. Hanowsky reported that she filed a supplement on Friday (9/25/15). The 2 petitions for letters of administration were continued to November 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chien. (Not Reported)
